add-inputs.hint: Click the I/O tab in the left panel, then drag two Input Switch elements onto the canvas and place them to the left of the AND gate.
add-inputs.instruction: Add two Input Switch elements to the canvas

add-output.hint: Drag an LED from the I/O tab and place it to the right of the AND gate. Then connect the AND gate output port (right side) to the LED input port (left side).
add-output.instruction: Add an LED and connect it to the AND gate output

connect-inputs.hint: Click and drag from an Input Switch output port (right side) to one of the AND gate input ports (left side). Repeat for the second switch.
connect-inputs.instruction: Connect both Input Switches to the AND gate

basic-and-gate.description: Learn to place logic gates, add inputs and outputs, and wire them together.

place-and-gate.hint: Click the Gates tab in the left panel, then drag the AND gate onto the canvas.
place-and-gate.instruction: Place an AND gate on the canvas

basic-and-gate.title: Building an AND Gate Circuit

toggle-observe.hint: Click an Input Switch to toggle it. The LED only lights up when BOTH switches are ON. That is because AND outputs 1 only when all inputs are 1.
toggle-observe.instruction: Toggle the switches and observe the LED — this is AND logic!

add-inputs.hint: Click the I/O tab in the left panel, then drag two Input Switch elements onto the canvas and place them to the left of the OR gate.
add-inputs.instruction: Add two Input Switch elements to the canvas

add-not-gate.hint: Drag the wire between the OR gate and the LED to remove it.
add-not-gate.instruction: Disconnect the LED from the OR gate, then connect the OR gate to a NOT gate and the NOT gate to the LED

add-output.hint: Drag an LED from the I/O tab and place it to the right of the OR gate. Then connect the OR gate output port (right side) to the LED input port (left side).
add-output.instruction: Add an LED and connect it to the OR gate output

connect-inputs.hint: Click and drag from an Input Switch output port (right side) to one of the OR gate input ports (left side). Repeat for the second switch.
connect-inputs.instruction: Connect both Input Switches to the OR gate

basic-or-not-gates.description: Learn to use the OR and NOT gates.

place-or-gate.hint: Click the Gates tab in the left panel, then drag the OR gate onto the canvas.
place-or-gate.instruction: Place an OR gate on the canvas

basic-or-not-gates.title: More basic gates: OR and NOT

toggle-observe-nor.instruction: The NOT gate inverts the signal. Now, the circuit will output 1 only when none of the inputs are 1

toggle-observe-or.instruction: Play around with the inputs and notice how the OR gate outputs 1 when any of its inputs are 1

combinational-mode.hint: Click Edit → Combinational in BeWavedDolphin's menu bar. This fills the Clock row with all possible values and computes the LED output for each — a full truth table in one click.

connect-clock-led.hint: Click and drag from the Clock's output port (right side) to the LED's input port (left side).
connect-clock-led.instruction: Connect the Clock output to the LED input

clock-led-waveform.description: Build a minimal Clock–LED circuit and explore the waveform viewer: combinational mode and manual cell editing.

open-waveform.hint: BeWavedDolphin shows your circuit signals as rows. The Clock row is the input, and the LED row is the output. Each column is one simulation time step.
open-waveform.instruction: The waveform viewer is now open — take a look!

place-clock.hint: Click the I/O tab on the left panel, then drag the Clock element onto the canvas.
place-clock.instruction: Place a Clock element on the canvas

place-led.hint: Still in the I/O tab, drag an LED element onto the canvas to the right of the Clock.
place-led.instruction: Place an LED on the canvas

clock-led-waveform.title: Clock and LED: Waveform Basics

toggle-cell.hint: Double-click any cell in the Clock row to flip it between 0 and 1. Watch the LED row update instantly to reflect the new output.

add-demux.hint: Your circuit should have two Input Switches, two LEDs and one DEMUX circuit at this point
add-demux.instruction: Add a DEMUX circuit and connect the Input Switches to its In and S0 ports. Then connect each of the DEMUX's outputs to a different LED

add-inputs.hint: Click the I/O tab in the left panel, then drag two Input Switch elements onto the canvas and place them to the left of the MUX circuit.
add-inputs.instruction: Add three Input Switch elements to the canvas

add-output.hint: Drag an LED from the I/O tab and place it to the right of the MUX circuit. Then connect the MUX circuit output port (right side) to the LED input port (left side).
add-output.instruction: Add an LED and connect it to the MUX circuit output

connect-inputs.hint: Click and drag from an Input Switch output port (right side) to one of the MUX gate input ports (left side). Repeat for the second switch. Then, connect the third Input Switch to the MUX selection port (bottom)
connect-inputs.instruction: Connect all Input Switches to the MUX circuit

combinational-circuits-one.description: Learn about the MUX and DEMUX circuits.

explain-demux-select.instruction: The S0 port at the bottom of the DEMUX selects which output will receive the input's signal. When S0 = 0, Out0 = In0. When S0 = 1, Out1 = In0

explain-mux-select.instruction: The S0 port at the bottom of the MUX selects which input is routed to the output. When S0 = 0, the signal from Input 0 is sent to the output. When S0 = 1, the signal from Input 1 is sent to the output.

place-mux.hint: Click the Combinational tab in the left panel, then drag the MUX circuit onto the canvas.
place-mux.instruction: Place a MUX circuit on the canvas

prepare-demux.hint: Your circuit should have two Input Switches and two LEDs at this point
prepare-demux.instruction: Delete the MUX circuit and one of the Input Switches. Then add another LED

combinational-circuits-one.title: Combinational Circuits I: MUX and DEMUX

add-cin-and-gates.hint: Connect the outputs of the Input Switches into the inputs of the gates.
add-cin-and-gates.instruction: Add another Input Switch to the canvas and give it the label Cin. Then add an XOR gate, an AND gate and an OR gate

add-half-adder-gates.hint: Use the Gates tab to add the XOR and AND gates, then use the I/O tab to add two LEDs.
add-half-adder-gates.instruction: Add an XOR gate, an AND gate, and two LEDs to the canvas. Then connect each gate to a different LED

add-inputs.hint: Click the I/O tab in the left panel, then drag three Input Switch elements onto the canvas. The third Input Switch will be used later as the carry-in (Cin).
add-inputs.instruction: Add two Input Switch elements to the canvas

combinational-circuits-three.description: Learn how to build an adder circuit.

explain-full-adder.instruction: This circuit forms a full adder. Unlike a half adder, a full adder accepts a carry-in (Cin) from a previous addition and produces a carry-out (Cout) for the next stage. By connecting the Cout of one full adder to the Cin of another, multiple full adders can be chained together to add binary numbers of any length

label-half-adder-outputs.hint: Click on an LED and type in the label text box at the bottom left of the screen to give an LED a label.
label-half-adder-outputs.instruction: Give the LED connected to the XOR gate the label Sum, then give the LED connected to the AND gate the label Cout. This circuit forms a half adder, which adds two single-bit inputs

combinational-circuits-three.title: Combinational Circuits III: Adder

wire-full-adder.hint: Remove the old connections to the LEDs to be able to add the new ones.
wire-full-adder.instruction: Connect the old XOR gate and the Cin switch to the new XOR gate, then connect the new XOR gate to the Sum output. Also connect the old XOR gate and the Cin switch to the new AND gate. Finish by connecting both AND gates to the OR gate and the OR gate into Cout

wire-half-adder.hint: Connect the outputs of the Input Switches into the inputs of the gates.
wire-half-adder.instruction: Connect the two Input Switches to the XOR gate and the AND gate. Each Input Switch should connect to both gates. The XOR gate produces the Sum output, while the AND gate produces the Carry output

add-and-gates-leds.hint: Use the gates tab in the left panel to add the AND gates and the I/O tab to add the LEDs.
add-and-gates-leds.instruction: Add four AND gates and four LEDs to the canvas, then connect each AND gate to one of the LEDs

add-inputs.hint: Click the I/O tab in the left panel, then drag two Input Switch elements onto the canvas.
add-inputs.instruction: Add two Input Switch elements to the canvas

add-not-gates.hint: Click the gates tab in the left panel, then drag two NOT gate elements onto the canvas and connect the Input Switches into them.
add-not-gates.instruction: Add two NOT gates to the canvas and connect each Input Switch into a separate NOT gate

combinational-circuits-two.description: Learn how to build a 2-to-4 Decoder.

explain-decoder.instruction: For every specific combination of input bits, only one corresponding output line is activated while all others remain inactive

combinational-circuits-two.title: Combinational Circuits II: Decoder

wire-decoder.hint: Each AND gate should receive one signal from each input. Use the original Input Switch signal for a 1 and the corresponding NOT gate output for a 0..
wire-decoder.instruction: Connect the outputs of the NOT gates and the Input Switches to the AND gates so each AND gate represents one input combination

add-d-latch.hint: The D latch stores a single input value and avoids the invalid input combination of an SR latch.
add-d-latch.instruction: Remove the SR latch while keeping the Input Switches and LEDs. Then add a D latch

add-sr-latch.hint: Use the Memory tab to add an SR latch. Then use the I/O tab to add two Input Switches and two LEDs.
add-sr-latch.instruction: Add an SR latch, two Input Switches, and two LEDs to the canvas

explain-d-latch.instruction: Toggle the Input Switches and observe the D latch. When Enable is 1, the output follows the D input. When Enable is 0, the latch remembers its previous value

explain-sr-latch.instruction: Toggle the Input Switches and observe how the SR latch stores its state. Setting the S input to 1 causes the Q output to become 1, while setting the R input to 1 resets Q to 0. When both inputs return to 0, the latch remembers its last state until it is set or reset again

wire-d-latch.hint: The D input is the value to be stored. The Enable input determines when the latch updates its stored value.
wire-d-latch.instruction: Connect one Input Switch to the D input and the other to the Enable (E) input. Connect the Q and Q̅ outputs to the LEDs

wire-sr-latch.hint: Connect one Input Switch to the Set (S) input and the other to the Reset (R) input. Connect each output of the latch to a different LED.
wire-sr-latch.instruction: Connect the Input Switches to the S and R inputs of the SR latch. Then connect the Q and Q̅ outputs to the LEDs

add-flipflops.hint: Use the Memory tab to add the T flip-flops and a Clock. Then use the I/O tab to add three Input Switches and three LEDs.
add-flipflops.instruction: Add three T flip-flops, three Input Switches, a Clock, and three LEDs to the canvas

sequential-circuits-three.description: Learn how to build a counter circuit.

explain-counter.instruction: Turn all three Input Switches on and start the Clock. Observe the LEDs as the counter runs. The first LED changes state every clock pulse, the second changes every two pulses, and the third changes every four pulses, displaying a repeating 3-bit binary count from 000 to 111

sequential-circuits-three.title: Sequential Circuits III: Counter

wire-clock.hint: The first flip-flop receives the external clock signal.
wire-clock.instruction: Connect the Clock to the Clk input of the first T flip-flop

wire-outputs.hint: Each LED represents one bit of the counter, with the first LED being the least significant bit.
wire-outputs.instruction: Connect the Q output of each flip-flop to a different LED

wire-ripple-clock.instruction: Connect the Q output of the first flip-flop to the Clk input of the second flip-flop, then connect the Q output of the second flip-flop to the Clk input of the third flip-flop. Each flip-flop is clocked by the output of the previous one, creating a ripple counter

wire-t-inputs.hint: Turn all three Input Switches on. A T flip-flop toggles only when its T input is 1.
wire-t-inputs.instruction: Connect each Input Switch to the T input of a different flip-flop

add-d-flipflop.hint: Use the Memory tab to add a D flip-flop and a Clock. Then use the I/O tab to add two Input Switches and two LEDs.
add-d-flipflop.instruction: Add a D flip-flop, two Input Switches, a Clock, and two LEDs to the canvas

add-jk-flipflop.hint: The JK flip-flop has J and K inputs instead of a D input. Leave the Clear and Preset inputs disconnected.
add-jk-flipflop.instruction: Replace the D flip-flop with a JK flip-flop while keeping the Clock, Input Switches, and LEDs

add-t-flipflop.hint: A T flip-flop only requires a single input in addition to the clock.
add-t-flipflop.instruction: Replace the JK flip-flop with a T flip-flop and remove one of the Input Switches

explain-clear-preset.instruction: Connect the second Input Switch to either the Clear or Preset input and observe its effect. Unlike the D input, the Clear and Preset inputs act immediately without waiting for a clock edge. Clear forces Q to 0, while Preset forces Q to 1

explain-d-flipflop.instruction: Toggle the D Input Switch and observe the D flip-flop. The value on the D input is copied to the Q output only on the active clock edge. Between clock edges, the output remains unchanged

explain-jk-flipflop.instruction: Toggle the Input Switches and observe the JK flip-flop. When J = K = 0, the output holds its current state. When J = 1 and K = 0, the output is set. When J = 0 and K = 1, the output is reset. When J = K = 1, the output toggles on every active clock edge

explain-t-flipflop.instruction: Toggle the Input Switch and observe the T flip-flop. When T = 0, the output keeps its current state. When T = 1, the output toggles on every active clock edge. T flip-flops are commonly used to build binary counters and frequency dividers

wire-d-flipflop.hint: Leave the Clear and Preset inputs disconnected for now.
wire-d-flipflop.instruction: Connect one Input Switch to the D input, the Clock to the Clk input, and the Q and Q̅ outputs to the LEDs. The D input provides the value to be stored, while the Clock determines when the value is stored

wire-jk-flipflop.hint: The JK flip-flop changes its output only on the active clock edge.
wire-jk-flipflop.instruction: Connect the Input Switches to the J and K inputs, the Clock to the Clk input, and the Q and Q̅ outputs to the LEDs

wire-t-flipflop.hint: Leave the Clear and Preset inputs disconnected.
wire-t-flipflop.instruction: Connect the remaining Input Switch to the T input, the Clock to the Clk input, and the Q and Q̅ outputs to the LEDs

canvas.body: Build your circuit here. Drag components from the palette, then connect them by clicking and dragging from an output port (right side of a component) to an input port (left side of another).
canvas.title: The Canvas

combinational-mode.body: The highlighted button (Combinational) was just triggered automatically — it fills the input rows with every possible 0/1 combination, giving you a complete truth table in one click.
combinational-mode.title: Combinational Mode

component-palette.body: This panel lists all available components — logic gates, inputs, outputs, flip-flops, and more. Components are organized by category using the tabs at the top. Click a tab to browse its category.
component-palette.title: Component Palette

ui-overview.description: A guided walkthrough of the main interface — palette, canvas, toolbar, and properties panel.

editing-cells.body: You can also edit the waveform by hand. Double-click any cell in the Clock row to flip it between 0 and 1 — the LED row updates instantly to reflect the new output.
editing-cells.title: Editing Cells Manually

element-properties.body: When you select a component on the canvas, its properties appear here. You can set labels, colors, trigger types, and other component-specific settings. Try selecting a placed component to see its options.
element-properties.title: Element Properties

logic-gates.body: The Gates tab contains AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, and other fundamental logic gates. Drag any gate from this list onto the canvas to add it to your circuit.
logic-gates.title: Logic Gates

search-bar.body: Use the search bar to quickly find any component by name. As you type, a filtered list appears below showing matching components across all categories.
search-bar.title: Search Bar

ui-overview.title: wiRedPanda Interface Tour

waveform-table.body: The waveform table shows every signal in your circuit. The Clock row is the input; the LED row is the computed output. Each column is one simulation time step — you can see exactly how the output follows the input.
waveform-table.title: Reading the Waveform Table

waveform-viewer.body: wiRedPanda includes a waveform viewer called BeWavedDolphin. It shows how every signal in your circuit changes over time — inputs as rows at the top, outputs below, with each column representing one simulation time step. Click the highlighted toolbar button to open it.
waveform-viewer.title: Waveform Viewer

welcome.body: wiRedPanda is a digital logic circuit simulator. You can build circuits by placing components and wiring them together. This tour will show you the main parts of the interface.
welcome.title: Welcome to wiRedPanda!
